master.description: Main development tasks for HappyCow scraper crawling fix

tasks.title: Create Page Type Detection Utility
tasks.description: Build a utility function to automatically detect whether a URL is a city listing page or individual restaurant page
tasks.details:
Create a robust URL pattern matching system that can distinguish between:
- City pages: `/north_america/usa/state/city/` patterns
- Restaurant pages: `/reviews/restaurant-name-id/` patterns
- Handle edge cases like `/update` suffixes and URL variations

Implementation should include:
- Regular expressions for URL pattern matching
- Enum or constants for page types
- Comprehensive test coverage for URL classification
- Error handling for malformed URLs
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests covering all known URL patterns, edge cases, and malformed URLs. Test with actual HappyCow URLs from both page types.
acceptanceCriteria: Correctly identifies city listing pages with 100% accuracy
Correctly identifies restaurant pages with 100% accuracy
Handles edge cases like /update suffixes
Returns appropriate error for malformed URLs
Performance under 1ms per URL classification

tasks.title: Define CSS Selector Strategy by Page Type
tasks.description: Create page-type-specific CSS selector configurations for optimal crawling
tasks.details:
Define and implement CSS selector strategies:

**City Listing Pages:**
- Primary selectors: `.card-listing, .venue-list-item`
- Fallback selectors: `.venue-item`
- Error detection: `.no-results, .captcha`

**Restaurant Pages:**
- Primary selectors: `.restaurant-name, .venue-details, h1`
- Content selectors: `.review-content, .venue-info, .contact-info`
- Error detection: `.not-found, .error-message`

Implementation should include:
- Configuration objects for each page type
- Fallback selector strategies
- Timeout specifications per page type
tasks.testStrategy: Test selectors against actual HappyCow pages. Verify elements exist and return expected content. Test fallback scenarios.
acceptanceCriteria: City page selectors find restaurant listings successfully
Restaurant page selectors find restaurant details successfully
Fallback selectors work when primary selectors fail
Error detection selectors properly identify blocking/errors
Configuration is easily maintainable and extensible

tasks.title: Implement Conditional Crawl4AI Configuration
tasks.description: Refactor scraper to use different crawl4ai configurations based on detected page type
tasks.details:
Modify the scraper's crawl4ai configuration to be page-type-aware:

**Implementation Requirements:**
- Create separate CrawlerRunConfig objects for city vs restaurant pages
- Implement page type detection before crawling
- Apply appropriate wait_for selectors based on page type
- Handle timeout and retry logic per page type

**Configuration Changes:**
- City pages: Use existing working selectors
- Restaurant pages: Use restaurant-specific selectors
- Remove one-size-fits-all approach
- Add error handling for unsupported page types
tasks.testStrategy: Test with known failing restaurant URLs and working city URLs. Verify crawl4ai returns Success: True for both page types.
acceptanceCriteria: City listing pages continue to work without regression
Restaurant pages now return Success: True from crawl4ai
Page type detection is called before every crawl
Appropriate selectors are used for each page type
Error handling works for unknown page types

tasks.title: Add Regression Testing for City Listing Pages
tasks.description: Ensure existing city listing functionality remains intact during refactoring
tasks.details:
Create comprehensive regression tests to prevent breaking existing functionality:

**Test Coverage:**
- Dallas, Texas listing page (known working)
- Los Angeles, California listing page (known working)
- Empty city pages (no restaurants)
- Pages with pagination
- Performance benchmarks

**Test Implementation:**
- Automated test suite with before/after comparisons
- Performance regression detection
- HTML content validation
- Restaurant extraction validation
tasks.testStrategy: Run tests against current working implementation, then after each change. Compare results for any regressions.
acceptanceCriteria: All existing city pages continue to work
Performance within 10% of baseline
Same number of restaurants extracted
No new errors or exceptions
HTML content quality maintained

tasks.title: Test Restaurant Page Crawling with New Configuration
tasks.description: Validate that restaurant pages now successfully crawl with the new page-type-aware configuration
tasks.details:
Test the new configuration against known failing restaurant URLs:

**Test URLs:**
- https://www.happycow.net/reviews/tane-vegan-izakaya-los-angeles-467122/update
- https://www.happycow.net/reviews/la-vegan-los-angeles-14477
- https://www.happycow.net/reviews/my-vegan-los-angeles-119792

**Validation Points:**
- crawl4ai returns Success: True
- HTML content is retrieved (>100K characters)
- Restaurant details are extractable
- No timeout errors
- Performance within acceptable limits (30s max)
tasks.testStrategy: Automated tests against the specific failing URLs. Manual verification of extracted restaurant data quality.
acceptanceCriteria: All test restaurant URLs return Success: True
Restaurant details can be extracted from HTML
No timeout or blocking errors
Performance under 30 seconds per page
Extracted data matches manual verification

tasks.title: Implement Fallback Selector Strategy
tasks.description: Add fallback logic when primary selectors fail to find content
tasks.details:
Implement intelligent fallback when primary selectors don't work:

**Fallback Strategy:**
- Try primary selectors first
- If no elements found, try fallback selectors
- If still no elements, try generic selectors (body, main)
- Log which selector strategy worked for future optimization

**Implementation:**
- Retry logic with different selector sets
- Logging and metrics collection
- Graceful degradation to generic selectors
- Performance optimization to avoid unnecessary retries
tasks.testStrategy: Test with pages that might have different DOM structures. Verify fallback selectors are used when needed.
acceptanceCriteria: Primary selectors are always tried first
Fallback selectors are used when primary fails
Generic selectors work as last resort
Logging shows which strategy was used
No performance degradation from fallback logic

tasks.title: Enhanced Error Handling and Logging
tasks.description: Improve error messages and logging to distinguish between page type issues and actual crawling failures
tasks.details:
Enhance error handling to provide better debugging information:

**Error Categories:**
- Page type detection failures
- CSS selector timeout failures
- Network/crawling failures
- Content extraction failures

**Logging Improvements:**
- Log detected page type for each URL
- Log which selector strategy was used
- Log performance metrics per page type
- Log detailed error context for failures

**Error Messages:**
- Clear distinction between different failure types
- Actionable error messages for debugging
- Context about page type and selectors used
tasks.testStrategy: Test error scenarios and verify error messages are clear and actionable. Check logs contain sufficient debugging information.
acceptanceCriteria: Error messages clearly indicate failure type
Logs show page type detection results
Logs show selector strategy used
Performance metrics are logged
Error context is sufficient for debugging

tasks.title: Performance Optimization for Page Type Detection
tasks.description: Optimize page type detection to minimize performance impact on crawling
tasks.details:
Optimize the page type detection for minimal performance impact:

**Optimization Strategies:**
- Cache page type detection results
- Optimize regex patterns for speed
- Minimize string operations
- Profile and benchmark detection performance

**Implementation:**
- LRU cache for URL pattern results
- Compiled regex patterns
- Performance benchmarking
- Memory usage optimization
tasks.testStrategy: Performance benchmarks before and after optimization. Memory usage profiling. Load testing with many URLs.
acceptanceCriteria: Page type detection under 1ms per URL
Memory usage remains stable
Cache hit rate >90% in typical usage
No memory leaks in long-running processes
Overall crawling performance not impacted

tasks.title: Create Integration Test Suite
tasks.description: Develop comprehensive integration tests covering both page types and edge cases
tasks.details:
Create a comprehensive integration test suite:

**Test Coverage:**
- End-to-end city listing scraping
- End-to-end restaurant page scraping
- Mixed page type scenarios
- Error handling and recovery
- Performance under load

**Test Implementation:**
- Automated test suite with CI/CD integration
- Mock and real URL testing
- Performance regression testing
- Error scenario testing
- Load testing capabilities
tasks.testStrategy: Run full integration tests in CI/CD pipeline. Test against live HappyCow URLs and mock scenarios.
acceptanceCriteria: All integration tests pass consistently
Tests cover both page types comprehensively
Error scenarios are properly tested
Performance benchmarks are maintained
Tests can run in CI/CD environment

tasks.title: Update Documentation and Code Comments
tasks.description: Document the new page type detection and conditional crawling logic
tasks.details:
Update documentation to reflect the new page-type-aware crawling:

**Documentation Updates:**
- Code comments explaining page type detection
- README updates for new crawling behavior
- API documentation for new functions
- Troubleshooting guide for common issues

**Content Requirements:**
- Clear explanation of page type detection logic
- Examples of different URL patterns
- Selector strategy documentation
- Error handling and debugging guide
tasks.testStrategy: Review documentation for completeness and accuracy. Verify examples work as documented.
acceptanceCriteria: Code is well-commented and self-documenting
README accurately describes new functionality
API documentation is complete and accurate
Troubleshooting guide helps resolve common issues
Examples in documentation work correctly

tasks.title: Deploy and Monitor Initial Implementation
tasks.description: Deploy the page-type-aware crawling and monitor for issues
tasks.details:
Deploy the new implementation with monitoring:

**Deployment Strategy:**
- Gradual rollout starting with test URLs
- Monitor success/failure rates by page type
- Performance monitoring and alerting
- Rollback plan if issues arise

**Monitoring Requirements:**
- Success rate metrics per page type
- Performance metrics (response time, memory usage)
- Error rate and error type tracking
- Page type detection accuracy metrics
tasks.testStrategy: Monitor deployment in production environment. Track metrics and respond to any issues quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: Deployment completes without errors
City page success rate maintains >95%
Restaurant page success rate achieves >95%
Performance metrics within acceptable ranges
No critical errors or system instability

tasks.title: Post-Deployment Optimization and Tuning
tasks.description: Optimize and tune the implementation based on real-world usage data
tasks.details:
Optimize the implementation based on production data:

**Optimization Areas:**
- Selector effectiveness analysis
- Timeout optimization per page type
- Error handling improvements
- Performance tuning based on real usage

**Data Analysis:**
- Analyze which selectors work best in practice
- Identify common failure patterns
- Optimize timeout values based on real performance
- Improve error handling based on observed errors

**Implementation:**
- Selector strategy refinements
- Configuration parameter tuning
- Error handling improvements
- Performance optimizations
tasks.testStrategy: Analyze production metrics and logs. Test optimizations in staging before production deployment.
acceptanceCriteria: Selector strategies optimized based on real data
Timeout values tuned for optimal performance
Error handling covers observed failure patterns
Overall system performance improved from baseline
Success rates maintained or improved
